architecture_fit.note: Single attention head, one-hot embedding, no MLP — minimal delta from base_model.py; token-identity Q/K circuit genuinely implements wildcard skipping with anchor mass 1.0 at every span.

baseline_comparison.note: Two controls plus uniform reference: prev-token bigram strawman works at span 0 and collapses for span>=1, ablated circuit goes uniform — clean success-vs-failure contrast.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built circuit with an ablation (zero the one matching weight) that collapses behaviour to uniform — appropriate causal evidence for a synthetic circuit, stated honestly in README.

operating_range.note: Holds flat across the full span sweep 0..4 by construction (content matching), but demonstrated range is bounded by the fixed seq_len=16 task; no exploration beyond.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Explicitly hand-set W_Q/W_K weights, never trained — the mechanism is written out by hand.

visual_judgement.note: Sweep plot (anchor mass + log sharpness across span vs all controls) plus interactive per-position attention bar make the skip and its falsification directly visible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties the y-axis (target->anchor attention) and x-axis (wildcard span) directly to the goal's question and justifies the controls shown.

notes: A minimal, transparent hand-built single-head circuit that solves wildcard n-gram matching by token identity, with anchor mass 1.0 flat across all spans and a clean ablation + positional strawman supplying causal and baseline evidence. The headline robustness of ~1.0 partly relies on pushing SCALE=30 so leakage drops under the evaluator's 1e-8 sharpness floor, but this is disclosed and the underlying behaviour is genuinely faithful. Visuals and rationale are well-matched to the goal; mean of scored items lands in the 'good' band.
